title: TSL Market Availability Monitor — Paper Design

motivation.p26k_finding: TSL removed NPB games 3469930.1 and 3469931.1 from the pre-game betting list at 03:24Z and 04:55Z respectively, approximately 4.1–5.6 hours before game_time=09:00Z. The daemon was executing correctly (TSL polled every 15 minutes with force_closing=True), but the source contained no target game data during the closing window (07:00–09:00Z). This failure was invisible — no alert was generated.
motivation.gap_identified: No mechanism exists to detect when a game disappears from TSL before the closing window opens. Once disappeared, the system silently skips closing capture with no record of the disappearance event.
motivation.design_goal: Define a per-match tracking schema and alert classification logic that would detect early market withdrawals, enabling post-hoc forensics and future alerting.

detection_schema.description: Per-match tracking record written each time the TSL pre-game list is polled. One record per match_id per poll cycle.

game_time_utc.description: Scheduled game start time in UTC

first_seen_timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when match was first observed in TSL response

last_seen_timestamp.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent poll cycle where match appeared in TSL response

latest_seen_in_source.description: True if match was present in the most recent TSL poll response; False if absent

disappeared_at.description: UTC timestamp of the first poll cycle where match was absent after having been seen. Null if match is still present.

hours_before_game.description: Number of hours between disappeared_at and game_time_utc. Null if disappeared_at is null.

consecutive_absent_cycles.description: Number of consecutive poll cycles where match has been absent. Resets to 0 if match reappears.

source_name.description: Name of the data source polled

source_response_context.description: Brief summary of the TSL response context when disappearance was first detected (e.g. total games returned, league counts)

classification.description: Alert classification assigned once disappearance is confirmed. Null while match is still present.

TSL_MARKET_WITHDRAWAL_EARLY.trigger: match previously seen (first_seen_timestamp != null) AND latest_seen_in_source == false AND disappeared_at != null AND hours_before_game > 2.0
TSL_MARKET_WITHDRAWAL_EARLY.description: TSL removed the game from the pre-game betting list more than 2 hours before kickoff. Closing odds will NOT be captured via TSL for this game.
TSL_MARKET_WITHDRAWAL_EARLY.recommended_action: Log to monitor_state file. Flag in closing summary report. No automatic recovery (paper_only).

p26k_instances.game: 讀賣巨人 vs 養樂多燕子
廣島東洋鯉魚 vs 橫濱海灣之星

TSL_MARKET_NORMAL_REMOVAL.trigger: match previously seen AND latest_seen_in_source == false AND disappeared_at != null AND hours_before_game <= 2.0
TSL_MARKET_NORMAL_REMOVAL.description: TSL removed the game within 2 hours of kickoff. This is consistent with normal pre-game market closure timing for most leagues.
TSL_MARKET_NORMAL_REMOVAL.recommended_action: Log to monitor_state file. No alert escalation needed.

TSL_MARKET_NEVER_SEEN.trigger: match_id expected (from schedule) but first_seen_timestamp == null AND game_time_utc < now
TSL_MARKET_NEVER_SEEN.description: A scheduled game was never observed in TSL responses during its expected availability window.
TSL_MARKET_NEVER_SEEN.recommended_action: Log. Investigate whether match was available on TSL at all.

monitor_state_file_design.schema: dict keyed by match_id, each value = latest MarketAvailabilityRecord
monitor_state_file_design.update_trigger: after each TSL poll cycle in tsl_snapshot.py (not modifying daemon; this is paper design only)
monitor_state_file_design.retention: current run only; not persisted across daemon restarts in paper design
monitor_state_file_design.note: Actual write location and persistence policy TBD at implementation phase.

integration_point_analysis.read_only_note: This design does NOT modify any existing code. The following describes where integration would occur IF implemented.

tsl_snapshot_py.proposed_hook: After parsing TSL response, compare returned match_ids against known expected match_ids. Write availability record for each expected match.
tsl_snapshot_py.lines_to_read: tsl_snapshot.py:255-285 (approximate region where games are filtered)

daemon_heartbeat.note: Availability state would be separate from daemon heartbeat. No heartbeat schema changes required.

p26k_reconstruction.description: Retroactive application of this schema to P26K data to validate design

note: This is a paper-only design artifact produced under P27A. No daemon, crawler, or ingestion code has been modified.
